data.prompt:
You are Archon Horizon's Horizon agent. You turn the blueprints into checked Lean: you pick the most valuable next piece of formalization and carry it as far as you can in one session. You self-scope from the recommendation, roadmap, and opened inbox below.

You have broad freedom at the workspace level: experiment, change the strategy entirely, try alternative formulations, edit blueprint material when proving teaches you something, and — when a task calls for it — work across the several projects it spans, or create and merge projects. The one hard rule is hygiene: stay within the projects in scope and the shared `references/`, don't leave stray directories or scratch files behind. The Ground agent and its review subagents run after you to reconcile, check math/blueprint alignment, and tidy — so prefer making progress over being cautious.

How to work:
- Read the relevant blueprint node and proof sketch before touching Lean.
- Run the project's build/check command (or the narrowest useful Lean check) and repair failures you introduce. Don't bury obligations under new `sorry`s unless a hint allows it.
- Use the inbox to comment, close, create, and message other projects with `--author horizon` (see the horizon-inbox skill). ALWAYS write item bodies, comments, and reports in Markdown — short paragraphs and `-` bullet lists (never one wall-of-text paragraph), `**bold**` for the key claim, and backticks for ids, lemma names, and files. Give every item a short title and a non-empty description. Comment at each key advance, and add a concise closing comment (runs, LOC, files) before you `complete` an item. Treat `[persistent]` items as standing rules; close `[temporary]` ones once consumed.
- Record durable dead ends as memory: `horizon inbox add --kind memory --to horizon --author horizon --body "..."`, so later sessions don't repeat them.
- You may delegate to subagents when it helps (see the Subagents section): split a wide search across parallel read-only subagents, hand mechanical checks to a cheaper-model subagent, or pull references. You are free to decide whether they fit the task.

# Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` — a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions — The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-inbox — Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox — list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, close, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- lean-check — Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors — use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag — Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) — declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects — via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch — Find existing lemmas/definitions in mathlib, workspace projects, and configured libraries with `horizon search`; use external Mathlib search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/Moogle/LSP) when broader Mathlib search is needed before writing proofs.
- project-git — How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace — root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
- subagents — How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work — spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.

Workspace: JacobianChallenge
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge at MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Write scope — you may edit ONLY paths matching these globs; treat everything else as read-only:
- MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/**
- references/**
Read scope: the entire workspace (cross-project reads are allowed).
Log/artifact directory: .archon-horizon/runs/0003/sessions/0003-horizon-T2

# Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint — Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end — keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug — Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run — missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors — applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor — Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier — keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber — Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever — Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] — Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task — starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper — then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and errors.
- blueprint-reviewer [read-only] — Check that the Lean declarations match their blueprint statements and the \uses dependency edges are correct.
- diff-auditor [read-only] — Audit the project diff from the last Horizon round for sound reasoning, no stray files, and no buried sorries.

Delegate by spawning a subagent **by name** through your engine's own subagent mechanism, giving it a focused directive: the slice/scope and the project it applies to. Spawn several in parallel when the work divides cleanly, then wait for and reconcile their reports. Read-only subagents are sandboxed off source edits and report through the `horizon inbox` CLI. You decide whether a subagent fits — use them when they help, skip them when they don't. Each subagent's descriptor pins its own model, usually a cheaper one for mechanical work: prefer delegating routine checks (diff review, reference lookup, lint) to those cheaper subagents and keep your own (more expensive) context for the hard reasoning. To add or change a subagent, edit its descriptor under `.archon-horizon/subagents/` — it recompiles on the next run.

# Latest Ground recommendation
This was saved as `recommendation.md` by the last Ground session; treat it as fresh guidance, not as a hard command if the live state contradicts it.
## Next
- **Human:** fix `I-0009`, then relaunch `horizon run AJC.fbc`. The T2 leaves are self-contained and fully documented in-file.
- No further Ground action possible until a Horizon session produces a diff.

# Recommended focus
Task: T2
Title: Prove the FBC lemma — flat base change (Stacks 02KH)
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Suggested scope: projects=Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge; files=AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/FlatBaseChange*,AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean

Close the **flat base change** route (Stacks **02KH**, Čech separated case) — the current PRIMARY directive in the project's `TO_USER.md`.

Prove, for flat $g$:
$$g^{*}R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;R^{i}f'_{*}\,g'^{*}\mathcal{F}.$$

Remaining open leaves:
- `cechComplex_baseChange_iso` (Stacks **02KG**, termwise affine base change) via `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso` in `Cohomology/FlatBaseChange`.
- `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (flat $g^{*}$ left-exact).

to finish `IsIso bareBC` in `Cohomology/CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional`. The affine-base separated case suffices for **Kleiman 4.8 Step 1**.

**Done when:** `lake build` green, no new axioms; roadmap item `AJC.fbc` advances/closes.

# Roadmap slice
- AJC.albanese [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Albanese / abelian-variety leg (merges from Albanese) - **Albanese / abelian-variety leg.** The Albanese universal property of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ and the rational-map-extension machinery, developed in `Albanese`, merge back here to supply the abelian-variety structure feeding `AJC.jacobian`.

- AJC.cech [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Čech R^i f_* engine (merged) - **Čech higher-direct-image engine (merged, sorry-free).**
$$H^{i}\big(\check{C}^{\bullet}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})\big)\;\cong\;R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\qquad(f\ \text{separated quasi-compact, finite affine cover}).$$
`cech_computes_higherDirectImage` + `pushPull` functoriality, proved in `Cech-Cohomology` and merged into AJC (axiom-clean). Residual 16 `sorry` are v4.31 migration-interim, mechanical.

- AJC.fbc [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Flat base change (Stacks 02KH) - **Flat base change (Stacks 02KH), Čech route.** For flat $g$:
$$g^{*}R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;R^{i}f'_{*}\,g'^{*}\mathcal{F}.$$
Homology machinery sorry-free (separated, no spectral sequence). Two open leaves: `cechComplex_baseChange_iso` (Stacks 02KG, termwise affine BC via `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso`) and `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (flat $g^{*}$ left-exact). See task **T2**.

- AJC.grquot [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged) - **Relative Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged, sorry-free).**
$$\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)(T)\;\cong\;\{\,V_{T}\twoheadrightarrow Q\ \text{locally free rank }d\,\}\quad(\texttt{Grassmannian.represents}).$$
Plus the SNAP section graded ring/module (Stacks 01CV) and glue-descent atlas. Merged from `GR-Quot-Closure`.

- AJC.jacobian [pending/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Jacobian = Albanese (top goal) - **North star.** For a smooth proper geometrically-irreducible curve $C/k$, the Jacobian is an abelian variety representing the degree-$0$ relative Picard functor and realizing the Albanese property:
$$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}(T)\;=\;\ker\!\big(\mathrm{Pic}(C\times_{k}T)\to\mathrm{Pic}(C)\big),\qquad \dim_{k}\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}=g.$$
Smooth of relative dimension $g$, proper, geometrically irreducible (`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`).

- AJC.linebundle [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Line-bundle comparison-iso / Pic^sharp group (A.1.c.sub) - **Line-bundle comparison-iso substrate (A.1.c.sub).** Gives $\mathrm{Pic}^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ its abelian-group law via the tensor/dual comparison
$$\mathcal{L}\otimes_{\mathcal{O}_C}\mathcal{L}^{-1}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;\mathcal{O}_{C}.$$
Finished in `Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso`; merges back into AJC via task **T1** (closes the 5 residual `TensorObjSubstrate`/`PullbackTensorComp` sorries).

- AJC.pic0av [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Pic^0 is an abelian variety (A.3) - **$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ is an abelian variety (A.3).** Kleiman §5 tangent-space identification, then structure:
$$T_{e}\,\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\;\cong\;H^{1}(C,\mathcal{O}_{C}),\qquad \dim=g.$$
Remaining: the $k$-linear comparison $\operatorname{Dual}_k(\mathfrak{m}_e/\mathfrak{m}_e^{2})\cong H^{1}(C,\mathcal O_C)$ (`tangentSpaceIso`); then un-invert `smooth` and `proper` (Chevalley–Rosenlicht) and geometric irreducibility.

- AJC.picrep [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Picard scheme representability cone - **Picard scheme representability (FGA).** The relative Picard functor is representable by a scheme locally of finite type:
$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable};\qquad \mathrm{Quot},\ \mathrm{Grass},\ \text{flattening stratification as inputs}.$$
Cone: `FGAPicRepresentability`, `QuotScheme` endgame, `FlatteningStratification`, `IdentityComponent`.

- AJC.substrate [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Foundational substrate (Kähler, rigidity, Abel–Jacobi) - **Foundational substrate (sorry-free).** Kähler differentials / cotangent ($\Omega_{C/k}$, cotangent iso, chart algebra), the rigidity lemma, genus theory, Abel–Jacobi scaffolding, and line-bundle coherence/pullback (`RelPicFunctor`, `RelativeSpec`).


# Opened inbox
(no accepted inbox items)

# Memory
- v4.31.0 migration fix patterns (Cech + GR push-pull/pullback proofs)

Effective repairs for the **v4.31.0 defeq-transparency-wall** breaks in these projects:

- **Port from the AJC copy first.** `MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/.../<same file>` is often already sorry-free (e.g. `PresheafCech.lean`, `CechSectionIdentificationBase.lean`). `diff` the subproject vs AJC; if they differ only in the sorry regions, copy the AJC file wholesale.
- **`set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in`** before a lemma fixes most "type mismatch under instances transparency" / stuck defeq. **Placement matters**: it must go BEFORE the docstring `/-- … -/`, never between docstring and `lemma` (that is a parse error: "unexpected token 'set_option'; expected 'lemma'"). Stacks with `set_option maxHeartbeats N in`.
- **`erw [Category.comp_id]` / `erw [Category.id_comp]`** closes residual `A ≫ 𝟙 = A` / `𝟙 ≫ A = A` when the `𝟙`'s object is only defeq (e.g. `(𝟭).obj M` vs `M`, or a `⋙`-form) so `simp`/`exact Category.comp_id _` fail. `erw` matches through the wall then auto-`rfl`.
- **`rw [reassoc_of% h1, reassoc_of% h2]` ordering**: cancel the INNER hom≫inv pair first so the outer pair becomes adjacent.
- **`subst h; simp <;> rfl`** for eqToHom-transport lemmas whose two sides reduce to defeq-but-not-syntactic `map = map`.
- **LSP is dead** on these full-`import Mathlib` files (server crashes / broken pipe). Iterate with `lake build <module>` and read the printed unsolved-goal state from the error output. `GlueDescent` ≈ 2.6 min/build; `CechHigherDirectImage` ≈ 8.5 min/build.
- **SOLVED (supersedes the earlier "two v4.31 sorrys remain hard" memory): both `rawPushPullMap_comp` (Cech) and `pullback_cast_compat` (GR) are now proved sorry-free.**

- **`CechHigherDirectImage.lean` `rawPushPullMap_comp`**: final transport is pure **term-mode**, no `rw` (avoids "motive not type correct"). Shape: `(congrArg (fun t => (pushforward p₁).map A_head ≫ t) he).trans (((Functor.map_comp _ _ _).symm.trans (congrArg (pushforward p₁).map ((Category.assoc _ _ _).trans INNER))).trans (Functor.map_comp _ _ _))`. `he` rewrites the outer over-triangle `eqToHom` to `(pushforward p₁).map eqInner`; `Category.assoc` fixes `(x≫y)≫z` vs `x≫(y≫z)` before `INNER`; `map_comp` folds/splits.

- **`GlueDescent.lean` `pullback_cast_compat`**: proved from the associativity solved-forms. Recipe: (1) `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in` BEFORE the docstring (the `pullbackComp` solved-forms unfold to the `⋙`-object form; else v4.31 transparency wall blocks matching). (2) tagged `pullbackComp_inv_comp_map_inv_app` + `pullbackComp_inv_comp_congr_hom_app` `@[reassoc]`. (3) phase-1 `simp only [pullbackComp_comp_fst_hom_app, pullback_map_inv_comp_hom_app_assoc, pullback_map_congr_inv_comp_hom_app_assoc, pullbackComp_inv_comp_congr_hom_app_assoc, pullbackComp_inv_comp_map_inv_app(_assoc), Category.assoc]` keeping congr as casts. (4) phase-2 `simp only [pullbackCongr_hom/inv_app_eqToHom, eqToHom_trans, eqToHom_trans_assoc, eqToHom_refl, Category.assoc, comp_id, id_comp]` (need `eqToHom_trans_assoc` for mid-chain). Variance: `pullbackComp x y : pullback y ⋙ pullback x ≅ pullback (x≫y)`.

- Iterate via `lake build <module>` reading the unsolved-goal state (LSP dead).

This is a ONE-SHOT headless session: when you produce your final report the session ENDS and you will NOT be re-invoked. Nothing calls you back when a background job finishes, and any background process you started is killed when the session exits. So you cannot 'kick off a build and wait to be notified' — that notification never comes here, and the build dies.
Therefore: if a build, subagent, monitor, or shell command produces a result your conclusion depends on, you MUST get that result WITHIN this session — run it in the FOREGROUND and block on it (read its exit code), or actively poll until it has definitively finished, before writing the report. A heavy `lake build` can take many minutes; budget for that and wait it out. If it genuinely cannot finish in time, do NOT imply success: record the unfinished build under `## Issues`, state what's still unknown, and leave the explicit next action — never write a conclusion that assumes a pending result.

Finish with a brief run-local report. Recommended sections: `## Summary`, `## Progress`, `## Issues`, and `## Next`; add or rename sections if another shape is clearer. Prefer short bullets, and keep each bullet around 20 words or fewer. Always mention bugs, build failures, broken proofs, suspicious code issues, blocked dependencies, unresolved assumptions, and checks that failed or were not run. For this Horizon session, make the report complete enough that a human or Ground can understand the session before opening raw logs.

- **D3′ line-bundle substrate (PTC) — DONE (iter-326).** `pullbackTensorMap_restrict` closed axiom-clean; the TensorObjSubstrate (DUAL + D3′) is complete. The remaining **comparison-iso CONSUMER** (D4′ chart-chase) is your **external subproject** `~/…/SubProjects/Line-Bundle_Comparison-Iso` — the loop leaves it untouched per your directive.

- **FBC (your PRIMARY 02KH directive) — keystone landed; route simplified + made sound; progressing autonomously.** iter-328 closed the cover-localization iso criterion `isIso_iff_isIso_restrict` (NEW file `Cohomology/ModulesCoverConservativity.lean`, axiom-clean). iter-329: found the open-immersion base-change lemma was UNSOUND as signed (arbitrary module, no quasicoherence — flat base change fails off the qcoh locus) and corrected it; adding affineness of the open `V` (the consumer already provides it) **collapsed the feared multi-hundred-LOC per-affine core to a single call of an existing proven brick**. Blueprint reworked + gate-clear; the loop now re-signs the chain + builds it to close `IsIso bareBC`. Affine-base separated case suffices for Kleiman 4.8 Step 1; override via `USER_HINTS.md` for general-S. No blocker for you.

- **v4.31.0 migration debt** (build GREEN): ~20–30 interim `sorry`s tagged `v4.31.0 ISOLATION`/`INTERIM` across `Cohomology/Cech*`, `Picard/{SectionGradedRing,GrassmannianQuot,GlueDescent,TensorObjSubstrate,QuotScheme}` — proofs complete pre-migration, now holes; restored when each file is next touched.

13	/- USER (2026-06-29): `cech_flatBaseChange` (Stacks 02KH) is the Kleiman-4.8 Step-1 prerequisite and the
14	   active target. PER USER, close it via the ČECH-TO-COHOMOLOGY SPECTRAL SEQUENCE: build the relative SS

16	   abstract `Algebra.Homology.SpectralObject.*` / `SpectralSequence.Basic` / `TotalComplex` (total of the
17	   Čech×resolution bicomplex; the Čech side = `cechComplexOnX`/`cech_computes_higherDirectImage`). The
18	   SS's base-change functoriality (E₂ = Čech cohomology of `R^q f_*`, base-changed via the termwise
19	   affine iso `cechComplex_baseChange_iso` / concrete-tilde route) yields FBC and lifts the separated
20	   case to general — replacing the walled termwise mate-calculus. Scope the SS as a new blueprint node
21	   `lem:cech_to_derived_pushforward_ss`. Full plan + anchors: `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` temporary hint. -/

23	/-!
24	# Unconditional `Rⁱ f_*` via Čech + flat base change (target-local roadmap)
25	
26	These two declarations are **target-local** content preserved across the
27	enrich merge of the `Cech-Cohomology` subproject (2026-06-18). They originally
28	lived at the tail of the target's `Cohomology/CechHigherDirectImage.lean`, which
29	was replaced wholesale by the source library's (more fundamental and more
30	complete) `CechHigherDirectImage.lean`. The source development does not package
31	these two specific lemmas, so they are reinstated here on top of the merged
32	`CechComplex` so that the target's blueprint scope (`def:cech_higher_direct_image`,
33	`lem:cech_flat_base_change`) is preserved and its `\uses{}` graph stays intact.
34	
35	* `cechHigherDirectImage` is sorry-free (a one-liner on the merged `CechComplex`).
36	* `cech_flatBaseChange` (Stacks 02KH) is now sorry-free *modulo* the following named leaves
37	  (currently 7 open `sorry`s, all blueprinted): the homology-side flat left-exactness
38	  `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (the abstract-left-adjoint wall); the two degreewise
39	  Beck–Chevalley leaves `cech_pushforward_baseChange_natIso` (S-level square along `g`,
40	  `naturality` residual) and `twisted_cech_nerve_iso` (X-level square along `g'`, `naturality`
41	  residual); the per-σ single-open S-level base change `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChange`
42	  (the affine-reduction heart — its LHS half is now LANDED via
43	  `pushPullObj_pushforward_iso_tilde_affine`, residual = RHS half + affine gap); the RHS leaf
44	  `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChanged_pushforward_iso_tilde`; the X-level per-σ Beck–Chevalley
45	  `twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma`; and its core open-immersion Beck–Chevalley
46	  `openImmersion_beckChevalley` — STAGE 1 (iter-326) is landed sorry-free (the pseudofunctor
47	  telescope `openImmersion_bc_telescope` + the bare mate `openImmersion_bareBC` collapse it to the
48	  single obligation `IsIso (openImmersion_bareBC … |>.app (p^* F))`); the residual STAGE 2 reduces,
49	  by the sectionwise criterion `Scheme.Modules.Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app`, to the Stacks-01HQ
50	  pullback-section formula for `pullback g'` (a Mathlib gap, shared with `canonicalBaseChangeMap`;
51	  affine-`U_σ` bypass via `pushPullObj_pushforward_iso_tilde_affine`). The abstract→affine
52	  bridge `pushPullObj_pushforward_iso_tilde_affine` (abstract `[IsAffine S]`, transported along
53	  `S.isoSpec`) is sorry-free and axiom-clean (iter-325). The
54	  monolithic cosimplicial base-change iso `e` was decomposed (iter-315) and packaged
55	  sorry-free as `cechComplex_baseChange_cosimplicialIso =
56	  cech_pushforward_baseChange_natIso ≪≫ (pushforward f')_*.mapIso twisted_cech_nerve_iso`.
57	  Both leaves reduce degreewise, through the product decomposition `pushPull_sigma_iso`
58	  to the per-σ open `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChange`, and thence through the shared
59	  affine brick `cech_degree_affine_baseChange` (this file), to the sorry-free affine
60	  termwise base change `affinePushforwardPullbackBaseChange` (`FlatBaseChange.lean`); the
61	  residual is the abstract→affine identification of the fibre-power push–pull objects
62	  with `tilde`-modules over `Spec` (see those leaves).
63	-/
64	
65	universe u
66	
67	open CategoryTheory Limits
68	
69	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
70	
71	open Scheme.Modules
72	
73	variable {S S' X X' : Scheme.{u}}
74	
75	/-- **Unconditional higher direct image via Čech.** For a separated quasi-compact
76	`f : X ⟶ S`, a finite affine open cover `𝒰` of `X`, and a quasi-coherent
77	`F : X.Modules`, the `i`-th higher direct image is the `i`-th cohomology of the
78	relative Čech complex. This needs **no** enough-injectives hypothesis on
79	`O_X`-modules: it is the cohomology of an explicit complex of quasi-coherent
80	sheaves. By `cech_computes_higherDirectImage` it agrees with the derived-functor
81	higher direct image wherever the latter is defined, and is independent of the
82	chosen affine cover up to canonical isomorphism. For `i = 0` one recovers the
83	ordinary pushforward `R⁰ f_* F = f_* F`. -/
84	noncomputable def cechHigherDirectImage (f : X ⟶ S) (𝒰 : X.OpenCover)
85	    (F : X.Modules) (i : ℕ) : S.Modules :=
86	  (CechComplex f 𝒰 F).homology i
87	
88	/-! ### Skeleton for `cech_flatBaseChange` (Stacks 02KH, separated case)
89	
90	The proof decomposes into the following pieces, assembled in `cech_flatBaseChange`:
91	
92	1. **Homology side — DONE (modulo flat left-exactness).** The pullback `g^*` is exact,
93	   so it commutes with `HomologicalComplex.homology`:
94	   * `pullback_preservesFiniteColimits` — free, `g^*` is a left adjoint;
95	   * `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` — **the one genuine homology-side gap**: `g`
96	     flat ⇒ `g^*` is left-exact (Mathlib has this affine-locally for `extendScalars`,
97	     `ModuleCat.preservesFiniteLimits_extendScalars_of_flat`, but not yet lifted
98	     through sheafification to `SheafOfModules.pullback`);
99	   * `pullback_preservesHomology` — then *derived* (no `sorry`) via
100	     `Functor.preservesHomologyOfExact`;
101	   * `mapHomologicalComplexHomologyIso` / `pullback_mapHC_homologyIso` — the
102	     complex-level upgrade of `ShortComplex.mapHomologyIso`, **sorry-free**.
103	2. **`cechComplex_baseChange_iso` (load-bearing, Stacks 02KG) — STILL OPEN.** Applying
104	   `g^*` degreewise to `Č•(𝒰, F)` recovers `Č•(𝒰', g'^* F)`. It is supplied (iter-315) by
105	   `cechComplex_baseChange_cosimplicialIso`, the whiskered composite of the cosimplicial
106	   Beck–Chevalley iso `cech_pushforward_baseChange_natIso` (degreewise → per-σ
107	   `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChange`, the affine-reduction heart routing through the
108	   altitude-2 bridge `pushPullObj_pushforward_iso_tilde` to the **sorry-free** affine
109	   termwise base change `affinePushforwardPullbackBaseChange` via the carved
110	   `restrictedCartesianAffinePushout` ring-pushout square) with the twisted-nerve
111	   identification `twisted_cech_nerve_iso` (per-σ `twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma`, the
112	   X-level open-immersion Beck–Chevalley `openImmersion_beckChevalley` over the
113	   cover-base-change identity `coverInterOpen_baseChange_eq`). The route uses the
114	   concrete-tilde non-mate brick, NOT the adjoint-mate machinery. This is the genuine open
115	   content of Stacks 02KG/02KH.
116	3. Assembly `cech_flatBaseChange` — **sorry-free**, reduces to 1 + 2.
117	
118	No spectral sequence is needed here: this is the *separated* case (`[IsSeparated f]`).
119	The Čech-to-cohomology spectral sequence enters only in the separated → general
120	quasi-separated promotion of Stacks 02KH, which is **not** this lemma. -/
121	
122	section HomologyComm
123	
124	variable {C D : Type*} [Category.{u} C] [Category.{u} D] [Preadditive C] [Preadditive D]
125	  [CategoryWithHomology C] [CategoryWithHomology D]
126	
127	/-- **Complex-level upgrade of `ShortComplex.mapHomologyIso`.** An additive functor `F`
128	that preserves homology commutes with `HomologicalComplex.homology`. The degree-`i`
129	short complex of `(F.mapHomologicalComplex c).obj K` is *definitionally* `F` applied to
130	the degree-`i` short complex `K.sc i` of `K` (both have `Xⱼ = F.obj (K.Xⱼ)` and
131	`d = F.map (K.d)`), so this is exactly `ShortComplex.mapHomologyIso (K.sc i) F`. -/
132	noncomputable def mapHomologicalComplexHomologyIso (F : C ⥤ D) [F.Additive]
133	    [F.PreservesHomology] {ι : Type*} {c : ComplexShape ι} (K : HomologicalComplex C c) (i : ι) :
134	    ((F.mapHomologicalComplex c).obj K).homology i ≅ F.obj (K.homology i) :=
135	  ShortComplex.mapHomologyIso (K.sc i) F
136	
137	end HomologyComm
138	
139	/-- **Flat base change has left-adjoint pullback**, hence `g^*` preserves finite
140	colimits (free: `g^* = pullback g` is a left adjoint). -/
141	instance pullback_preservesFiniteColimits (g : S' ⟶ S) :
142	    Limits.PreservesFiniteColimits (Scheme.Modules.pullback g) := inferInstance
143	
144	/-- **Flat ⇒ `g^*` is left-exact** *(STUB — the one genuine homology-side gap)*.
145	
146	The mathematical reduction is verified and worth recording. By
147	`SheafOfModules.pullbackIso`, the pullback factors as
148	`g^* ≅ forget ⋙ (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom ⋙ PresheafOfModules.sheafification)`.
149	Two of the three factors preserve finite limits *in Mathlib already*:
150	* `SheafOfModules.forget` — `SheafOfModules.forgetPreservesFiniteLimits` (it is a right
151	  adjoint to sheafification);
152	* `PresheafOfModules.sheafification` — the instance in
153	  `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Presheaf/Sheafification.lean` (sheafification is a
154	  left-exact reflector; needs `HasSheafify J AddCommGrpCat`, which holds for the scheme
155	  site since `X.Modules` is abelian).
156	
157	So the *only* irreducible content is that the **presheaf-level** pullback
158	`PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom` preserves finite limits when `g` is flat. Mathlib
159	defines this presheaf pullback purely as `(pushforward φ).leftAdjoint` with **no
160	pointwise description**; mathematically it is the inverse image `g⁻¹` (exact) followed
161	by extension of scalars along the flat ring map (left-exact, cf.
162	`ModuleCat.preservesFiniteLimits_extendScalars_of_flat`), but neither this factorisation
163	nor its left-exactness is packaged. Closing it is a genuine multi-hundred-LOC Mathlib
164	development (assembling it via `pullbackIso` additionally requires resolving the
165	`sheafification`/`HasSheafify` instances for the concrete scheme site). -/
166	/- USER (Stacks 02KH leaf 1/2): close via the reduction proved out in the docstring —
167	   transfer along `SheafOfModules.pullbackIso` and discharge `forget` + `sheafification`
168	   (both already preserve finite limits in Mathlib: `SheafOfModules.forgetPreservesFiniteLimits`,
169	   the `sheafification` instance in `Presheaf/Sheafification.lean`). The irreducible core is
170	   that `PresheafOfModules.pullback` is left-exact under flat (mathematically `g⁻¹` exact,
171	   then flat `extendScalars` left-exact via `ModuleCat.preservesFiniteLimits_extendScalars_of_flat`).
172	   Likely path: stalkwise (stalk of pullback = `extendScalars` of stalk + pointwise flat
173	   exactness). This is pure exactness of flat pullback — no Čech/cohomology or spectral
174	   sequence is involved here (those belong to the base-change *assembly*, not this leaf).
175	   Reference: Stacks 02KH (the flatness input). -/
176	instance pullback_preservesFiniteLimits (g : S' ⟶ S) [Flat g] :
177	    Limits.PreservesFiniteLimits (Scheme.Modules.pullback g) := sorry
178	
179	/-- **Flat ⇒ `g^*` preserves homology** — *derived* from left-exactness +
180	left-adjointness via `Functor.preservesHomologyOfExact`. No `sorry` of its own. -/
181	instance pullback_preservesHomology (g : S' ⟶ S) [Flat g] :
182	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).PreservesHomology := inferInstance
183	
184	/-- **`g^*` commutes with Čech homology** (flat exactness, complex level). **Sorry-free:**
185	a direct specialisation of `mapHomologicalComplexHomologyIso` to `g^* = pullback g`,
186	which is additive and (for `g` flat) preserves homology. -/
187	noncomputable def pullback_mapHC_homologyIso (g : S' ⟶ S) [Flat g]
188	    (K : CochainComplex S.Modules ℕ) (i : ℕ) :
189	    (((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj K).homology i
190	      ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj (K.homology i) :=
191	  mapHomologicalComplexHomologyIso (Scheme.Modules.pullback g) K i
192	
193	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — additive functors and the alternating coface complex
194	
195	The relative Čech complex `CechComplex` is, by construction
196	(`relativeCechComplexOfNerve`), the alternating coface-map cochain complex of a
197	cosimplicial object. The leaf-2 base change `cechComplex_baseChange_iso` must move the
198	degreewise pullback `g^*` *inside* this `alternatingCofaceMapComplex` construction. The
199	following two general declarations package exactly that move at the right (cosimplicial)
200	altitude, with no reference to schemes: an additive functor `F` commutes with the
201	alternating coface map complex, naturally in the cosimplicial variable. This is
202	Mathlib-absent and is the cosimplicial-natural-iso brick flagged in
203	`analogies/02kh-leaves-304.md` (step (b)). -/
204	
205	section AlternatingCoface
206	
207	open AlgebraicTopology
208	
209	variable {C D : Type*} [Category.{u} C] [Category.{u} D] [Preadditive C] [Preadditive D]
210	
211	/-- The degree-`n` differential of the alternating coface complex is the alternating sum
212	`objD`. Project-local unfolding lemma. -/
213	private theorem alternatingCofaceMapComplex_d
214	    (Y : CosimplicialObject C) (n : ℕ) :
215	    ((alternatingCofaceMapComplex C).obj Y).d n (n + 1)
216	      = AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD Y n := by
217	  simp only [alternatingCofaceMapComplex, AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.obj, CochainComplex.of_d]
218	
219	/-- **An additive functor commutes with the alternating coface differential.** For an
220	additive functor `F : C ⥤ D` and a cosimplicial object `Y`, applying `F` to the
221	degree-`n` alternating coface differential `objD Y n = ∑ᵢ (-1)ⁱ • Yδᵢ` equals the
222	alternating coface differential of the post-composed cosimplicial object `Y ⋙ F`. This is
223	`F.map_sum` together with `Functor.map_zsmul` (both available since `F` is additive).
224	Project-local. -/
225	theorem map_alternatingCofaceMapComplex_objD (F : C ⥤ D) [F.Additive]
226	    (Y : CosimplicialObject C) (i : ℕ) :
227	    F.map (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD Y i)
228	      = AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD
229	          (((CosimplicialObject.whiskering C D).obj F).obj Y) i := by
230	  rw [AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD, AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD, Functor.map_sum]
231	  apply Finset.sum_congr rfl
232	  intro k _
233	  rw [Functor.map_zsmul]
234	  rfl
235	
236	/-- **Additive functors commute with `alternatingCofaceMapComplex`.** For an additive
237	functor `F : C ⥤ D` and a cosimplicial object `Y` in `C`, applying `F` degreewise to the
238	alternating coface map cochain complex of `Y` yields the alternating coface map cochain
239	complex of the post-composed cosimplicial object `F ∘ Y`:
240	`F.mapHomologicalComplex (alternatingCofaceMapComplex Y) ≅ alternatingCofaceMapComplex (F ∘ Y)`.
241	The degreewise components are identities (the degree-`n` terms are `F.obj (Y.obj [n])` on
242	both sides) and the differential compatibility is `map_alternatingCofaceMapComplex_objD`.
243	This is the cosimplicial-altitude brick (step (b)) used to push `g^*` into the relative
244	Čech complex `relativeCechComplexOfNerve`. Project-local Mathlib supplement. -/
245	-- (v4.31.0: `CechToHigherDirectImage` also defines a public `mapAlternatingCofaceMapComplexIso`
246	-- — that file never compiled before the migration so the name clash was latent; now that it
247	-- builds, both being public collides at the root import. This copy is used only inside this file,
248	-- so mark it `private` to resolve the clash without rebuilding the 4.3 h `CechToHigherDirectImage`.)
249	private noncomputable def mapAlternatingCofaceMapComplexIso (F : C ⥤ D) [F.Additive]
250	    (Y : CosimplicialObject C) :
251	    (F.mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj ((alternatingCofaceMapComplex C).obj Y)
252	      ≅ (alternatingCofaceMapComplex D).obj
253	          (((CosimplicialObject.whiskering C D).obj F).obj Y) :=
254	  HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents (fun _ => Iso.refl _) (by
255	    rintro i j (rfl : i + 1 = j)
256	    have h : F.map (((alternatingCofaceMapComplex C).obj Y).d i (i + 1))
257	        = ((alternatingCofaceMapComplex D).obj
258	            (((CosimplicialObject.whiskering C D).obj F).obj Y)).d i (i + 1) := by
259	      rw [alternatingCofaceMapComplex_d, alternatingCofaceMapComplex_d]
260	      exact map_alternatingCofaceMapComplex_objD F Y i
261	    rw [Functor.mapHomologicalComplex_obj_d, h]
262	    erw [Category.id_comp, Category.comp_id])
263	
264	end AlternatingCoface
265	
266	/-- **The degreewise pullback of the relative Čech complex, in alternating-coface form.**
267	Specialising `mapAlternatingCofaceMapComplexIso` to `F = g^* = Scheme.Modules.pullback g`
268	and the push-forward cosimplicial object underlying `CechComplex f 𝒰 F`, this identifies
269	`g^*` applied degreewise to `Č•(𝒰, F)` with the alternating coface complex of the
270	cosimplicial object obtained by post-composing the dropped Čech nerve with `f_*` then `g^*`.
271	This is the first concrete step of the leaf-2 assembly `cechComplex_baseChange_iso`:
272	it moves the degreewise pullback inside the `alternatingCofaceMapComplex` construction,
273	reducing the remaining content to a (Beck–Chevalley) natural isomorphism of the underlying
274	cosimplicial objects `(g^* ∘ f_* ∘ nerve) ≅ (f'_* ∘ g'^* ∘ nerve')`. Project-local. -/
275	noncomputable def pullback_cechComplex_alternatingIso (f : X ⟶ S) (g : S' ⟶ S)
276	    (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
277	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj
278	        (CechComplex f 𝒰 F)
279	      ≅ (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex S'.Modules).obj
280	          (((CosimplicialObject.whiskering S.Modules S'.Modules).obj
281	              (Scheme.Modules.pullback g)).obj
282	            (((CosimplicialObject.whiskering X.Modules S.Modules).obj
283	                (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f)).obj
284	              (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj (CechNerve 𝒰 F)))) :=

291	Given such an `e`, `Functor.mapIso (alternatingCofaceMapComplex …)` transports it to a chain

297	noncomputable def cechComplex_baseChange_iso_of_cosimplicialIso
298	    (f : X ⟶ S) (g : S' ⟶ S) (f' : X' ⟶ S') (g' : X' ⟶ X)
299	    (h : IsPullback g' f' f g) (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules)
300	    (e : ((CosimplicialObject.whiskering S.Modules S'.Modules).obj
301	            (Scheme.Modules.pullback g)).obj

303	              (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f)).obj
304	            (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj (CechNerve 𝒰 F)))

306	            (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f')).obj
307	          (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj
308	            (CechNerve ((Scheme.Pullback.openCoverOfLeft 𝒰 f g).pushforwardIso

318	/-- **Degreewise affine reduction of the Čech base change** (Stacks 02KG; shared sub-lemma).
319	Fix a cosimplicial degree `p`. On the standard affine model of the cover the `(p+1)`-fold
320	fibre power `U_{i₀…iₚ} = U_{i₀} ×_X ⋯ ×_X U_{iₚ}` is `Spec` of the finite affine intersection
321	`A := A_{i₀} ⊗_R ⋯ ⊗_R A_{iₚ}` of the coordinate rings of the cover members, and the X-level
322	cartesian square defining the base change along `g'` restricts, over `U_{i₀…iₚ}`, to the affine
323	pushout (tensor) square of rings `(φ : R ⟶ A, ψ : R ⟶ R', ρ : A ⟶ B, σ : R' ⟶ B)`. On that
324	affine model the degreewise Beck–Chevalley comparison
325	```
326	  g'^*(p_* p^* F)  ≅  p'_* p'^*(g'^* F)        over U_{i₀…iₚ}
327	```
328	IS the affine termwise base change `affinePushforwardPullbackBaseChange`
329	(`FlatBaseChange.lean`), assembled from the concrete tilde dictionaries
330	`pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` / `pullback_spec_tilde_iso` and the commutative-algebra
331	cancellation `cancelBaseChange` — *not* the canonical adjoint mate `pushforwardBaseChangeMap`.
332	These affine identifications are natural with respect to the index-omission maps that generate
333	the cosimplicial structure of the nerve (each coface is the ring inclusion that inserts the
334	omitted tensor factor), since `affinePushforwardPullbackBaseChange` is natural in the ring.
335	
336	This is the **shared per-degree brick** consumed by both
337	`cech_pushforward_baseChange_natIso` and `twisted_cech_nerve_iso`: at each degree, after the
338	affine identification of the fibre power, the `app` field of either leaf is this isomorphism.
339	Sorry-free: a direct repackaging of the affine termwise base change at the intersection ring
340	`A`. Project-local; see blueprint `lem:cech_degree_affine_baseChange`. -/
341	noncomputable def cech_degree_affine_baseChange {R A R' B : CommRingCat.{u}}
342	    (φ : R ⟶ A) (ψ : R ⟶ R') (ρ : A ⟶ B) (σ : R' ⟶ B)
343	    (h : CategoryTheory.IsPushout φ ψ ρ σ) (M : ModuleCat.{u} A) :
344	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (Spec.map ψ)).obj
345	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ)).obj (tilde M)) ≅
346	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map σ)).obj
347	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Spec.map ρ)).obj (tilde M)) :=
348	  affinePushforwardPullbackBaseChange φ ψ ρ σ h M

350	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the abstract→affine `pushPullObj ≅ tilde` bridge

352	The two degreewise Beck–Chevalley leaves (`cech_pushforward_baseChange_natIso`,
353	`twisted_cech_nerve_iso`) reduce, on the affine model of the cover, to the sorry-free affine
354	brick `cech_degree_affine_baseChange`. The missing edge (Stacks 01I8 / 01BG) is the identification
355	of the abstract push–pull data of a fibre power with the `tilde`-model over `Spec`. We build it at
356	the two well-typed altitudes flagged in `analogies/fbc-pushpull-tilde-317.md`:
357	
358	* **altitude 1** (`pullbackRestrict_iso_tilde`): the restriction `p^* F = (V.ι)^* F` of a
359	  quasi-coherent `F` to an affine open `V` of `X`, *pushed forward along the whole-scheme iso*
360	  `V ≅ Spec Γ(X, V)` (`IsAffineOpen.isoSpec`), is `tilde` of its global sections over
361	  `Spec Γ(X, V)`. Quasi-coherence is preserved by pullback along the open immersion `V.ι`
362	  (`pullback_isQuasicoherent`/`isQuasicoherent_pullback_opens`) and by pushforward along the
363	  iso `isoSpec` (`pushforward_iso_preserves_qcoh`), so the affine structure theorem 01I8
364	  (`qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`, unconditional via the live instance
365	  `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_quasicoherent`) applies.
366	* **altitude 2** (`pushPullObj_pushforward_iso_tilde`): over the affine base `S = Spec R`, the
367	  pushed-forward push–pull object `f_*(p_* p^* F) = f_*((V.ι)_* (V.ι)^* F)` is `(Spec φ)_* (tilde N)`
368	  — collapse `f_* ∘ (V.ι)_*` to `(V.ι ≫ f)_*` by `pushforwardComp`, factor
369	  `V.ι ≫ f = isoSpec.hom ≫ Spec.map φ` (with `φ := Spec.preimage (fromSpec ≫ f)`,
370	  `Spec.map_preimage`), split off `(Spec.map φ)_*` by `pushforwardComp` again, and feed altitude 1
371	  through `(Spec.map φ)_*`. This is exactly the form the brick `cech_degree_affine_baseChange`
372	  consumes. See blueprint `lem:pullback_preserves_quasicoherent`, `lem:pushPullObj_iso_tilde`.

374	All ingredients are axiom-clean project infrastructure: `isQuasicoherent_pullback_opens`
375	(`CechTermAcyclic`), `pushforward_iso_preserves_qcoh` (`OpenImmersionPushforward`),
376	`qcoh_iso_tilde_sections` (`QcohTildeSections`, the unconditional 01I8 structure theorem).

382	`Y_n = U_{i₀} ∩ ⋯ ∩ U_{iₙ} ↪ X` is an open immersion); the general-morphism case is absent from both

393	pushed forward along the whole-scheme iso `isoSpec : V ≅ Spec Γ(X, V)`, is canonically isomorphic to

401	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward hV.isoSpec.hom).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.ι).obj F) ≅

412	`φ := Spec.preimage (fromSpec ≫ f) : R ⟶ Γ(X, V)` the ring map presenting the composite
413	`isoSpec.inv ≫ V.ι ≫ f = fromSpec ≫ f` as `Spec.map φ`, the pushed-forward push–pull object
414	`(pushforward f).obj (pushPullObj F (Over.mk V.ι))` is canonically isomorphic to
415	`(pushforward (Spec.map φ)).obj (tilde N)`.

418	`V.ι ≫ f = isoSpec.hom ≫ Spec.map φ` (from `Spec.map_preimage` and `isoSpec_inv_ι`) by
419	`pushforwardCongr`; split off `(Spec.map φ)_*` by `pushforwardComp` again (leaving the altitude-1

422	`cech_degree_affine_baseChange`.  Project-local; blueprint `lem:pushPullObj_iso_tilde` (altitude 2). -/
423	noncomputable def pushPullObj_pushforward_iso_tilde {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
424	    (f : X ⟶ Spec R) (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent)
425	    {V : X.Opens} (hV : IsAffineOpen V) :
426	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).obj (pushPullObj F (Over.mk V.ι)) ≅
427	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map (Spec.preimage (hV.fromSpec ≫ f)))).obj
428	        (tilde (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj
429	          ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward hV.isoSpec.hom).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.ι).obj F)))) :=
430	  have heq : V.ι ≫ f = hV.isoSpec.hom ≫ Spec.map (Spec.preimage (hV.fromSpec ≫ f)) := by
431	    rw [Spec.map_preimage, ← IsAffineOpen.isoSpec_inv_ι hV, Category.assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc]
432	  (pushforwardComp V.ι f).app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.ι).obj F) ≪≫
433	    (pushforwardCongr heq).app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.ι).obj F) ≪≫
434	    (pushforwardComp hV.isoSpec.hom (Spec.map (Spec.preimage (hV.fromSpec ≫ f)))).symm.app
435	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.ι).obj F) ≪≫
436	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map (Spec.preimage (hV.fromSpec ≫ f)))).mapIso
437	      (pullbackRestrict_iso_tilde F hF hV)

456	    {V : X.Opens} (hV : IsAffineOpen V) :
457	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).obj (pushPullObj F (Over.mk V.ι)) ≅
458	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward S.isoSpec.inv).obj
459	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map (Spec.preimage
460	            (hV.fromSpec ≫ (f ≫ S.isoSpec.hom))))).obj
461	          (tilde (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj
462	            ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward hV.isoSpec.hom).obj
463	              ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.ι).obj F))))) :=
464	  -- `collapse : (e_S⁻¹)_* ((f ≫ e_S)_* P) ≅ f_* P`, the `e_S` cancellation; take `.symm` to start
465	  -- from `f_* P`, then push the literal bridge through `(e_S⁻¹)_*`.
466	  ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward S.isoSpec.inv).mapIso
467	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp f S.isoSpec.hom).symm.app
468	          (pushPullObj F (Over.mk V.ι))) ≪≫
469	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp S.isoSpec.hom S.isoSpec.inv).app
470	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).obj (pushPullObj F (Over.mk V.ι))) ≪≫
471	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr S.isoSpec.hom_inv_id).app
472	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).obj (pushPullObj F (Over.mk V.ι))) ≪≫
473	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId S).app
474	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).obj (pushPullObj F (Over.mk V.ι)))).symm ≪≫
475	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward S.isoSpec.inv).mapIso
476	      (pushPullObj_pushforward_iso_tilde (f ≫ S.isoSpec.hom) F hF hV)

478	/-- **Čech intersection opens are affine** (separated case).  For a separated `f : X ⟶ S`
479	with `S` affine and an affine open cover `𝒰` of `X`, every finite nonempty fibre-power
480	intersection open `coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ = ⨅ k, (𝒰.f (σ k)).opensRange` is affine.  `X` is
481	separated over the terminal scheme (`f` separated and `S` affine — hence separated — so the
482	composite `terminal.from X = f ≫ terminal.from S` is separated), so the absolute diagonal of
483	`X` is a closed immersion, hence affine; finite intersections of affine opens of a scheme with
484	affine diagonal are affine (`IsAffineOpen.iInf`), and each member open is affine as the range of
485	an open immersion out of the affine `𝒰.X (σ k)` (`isAffineOpen_opensRange`).  This is the
486	affineness ingredient consumed by the affine-reduction heart `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChange`.
487	Project-local; blueprint `lem:cech_degree_affine_baseChange` (affineness side-condition). -/
488	theorem coverInterOpen_isAffine (f : X ⟶ S) [IsSeparated f] [IsAffine S]
489	    (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [∀ i, IsAffine (𝒰.X i)] {κ : Type} [Finite κ] [Nonempty κ]
490	    (σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀) : IsAffineOpen (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ) := by
491	  -- `X` is separated over the terminal scheme: `terminal.from X = f ≫ terminal.from S`, with
492	  -- `f` separated and `terminal.from S` separated (`S` affine ⟹ `S.IsSeparated`).
493	  haveI hsep : IsSeparated (terminal.from X) := by
494	    rw [← terminal.comp_from f]
495	    exact IsSeparated.comp_iff.mpr ‹IsSeparated f›
496	  -- hence the absolute diagonal is a closed immersion (⟹ affine), unlocking `IsAffineOpen.iInf`.
497	  haveI : IsClosedImmersion (pullback.diagonal (terminal.from X)) :=
498	    IsSeparated.isClosedImmersion_diagonal
499	  exact IsAffineOpen.iInf (fun k => isAffineOpen_opensRange (𝒰.f (σ k)))

501	/-- **Restriction of the cartesian square over an affine intersection open is a (ring) pushout**
502	(Stacks 02KG; carved block `lem:restricted_cartesian_affine_pushout`).  Restricting the global
503	cartesian square `X' = X ×_S S' → X` over the Čech fibre-power intersection open
504	`V = coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ↪ X` (open immersion `j_σ`) replaces `X` by `V` and `X'` by the fibre
505	product `X' ×_X V`, and the restricted square
506	```
507	  X' ×_X V --pullback.fst--> V
508	   |pullback.snd             |j_σ
509	   v                         v
510	  X'  --------g'------------> X
511	```
512	is cartesian.  This is the geometric half of the carved block: under `[IsSeparated f]`,
513	`[IsAffine S]`, `[IsAffine S']` and an affine cover, `V` is affine (`coverInterOpen_isAffine`)
514	and `X' ×_X V` is affine, so applying global sections turns this cartesian square of affines into
515	the cocartesian (pushout) square of rings `R → A_σ`, `R → R'`, `A_σ → A_σ ⊗_R R'` via the
516	affine-pullback ↔ ring-pushout equivalence `CommRingCat.isPushout_iff_isPushout`
517	(`lem:commRingCat_isPushout_iff_mathlib`, `\mathlibok`) — exactly the affine pushout square
518	consumed by `cech_degree_affine_baseChange`.  Sorry-free: the restricted square is a pullback by
519	construction (`IsPullback.of_hasPullback`).  Project-local; blueprint
520	`lem:restricted_cartesian_affine_pushout`. -/
521	theorem restrictedCartesianAffinePushout (g' : X' ⟶ X)
522	    (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) {κ : Type} (σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀) :
523	    IsPullback (pullback.snd g' (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)))
524	      (pullback.fst g' (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)))
525	      (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)) g' :=
526	  (IsPullback.of_hasPullback g' (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))).flip

528	/-- **LHS abstract → tilde for a single intersection open** (carved block
529	`lem:coverinter_lhs_iso_tilde`).  Over the affine base `S = Spec R`, for a separated `f : X ⟶ Spec R`,
530	an affine open-immersion cover `𝒰` and a finite nonempty multi-index `σ`, the intersection open
531	`V = coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ` is affine (`coverInterOpen_isAffine`) and the pushed-forward push–pull object
532	`f_*(pushPullObj F (Over.mk j_σ)) = f_*((j_σ)_* (j_σ)^* F)` is the affine pushforward
533	`(Spec φ)_*(tilde N)` of the tilde of its global sections, where `φ = Spec.preimage (fromSpec ≫ f)`

537	noncomputable def pushPullObj_coverInter_pushforward_iso_tilde {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
538	    (f : X ⟶ Spec R) [IsSeparated f]
539	    (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [∀ i, IsAffine (𝒰.X i)]
540	    (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent)
541	    {κ : Type} [Finite κ] [Nonempty κ] (σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀) :
542	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).obj
543	        (pushPullObj F (Over.mk (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)))) ≅
544	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map (Spec.preimage
545	          ((coverInterOpen_isAffine f 𝒰 σ).fromSpec ≫ f)))).obj
546	        (tilde (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj
547	          ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (coverInterOpen_isAffine f 𝒰 σ).isoSpec.hom).obj
548	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))).obj F)))) :=
549	  pushPullObj_pushforward_iso_tilde f F hF (coverInterOpen_isAffine f 𝒰 σ)

551	/-- **The base-changed sections are the tensor base change of `N`** (carved block
552	`lem:coverinter_baseChanged_module_iso_tensor`).  For the affine pushout square of rings cut out by
553	restricting the cartesian base-change square over the affine intersection open `V = Spec A_σ`
554	(`φ : R ⟶ A_σ`, `ψ : R ⟶ R'`, `ρ : A_σ ⟶ B`, `σ' : R' ⟶ B` with corner `B ≅ A_σ ⊗_R R'`), the
555	base-changed module of sections `N' = (j'_σ)^*((g')^* F)` over the affine `V' = Spec B` is, as a
556	`B`-module restricted to `R'` (resp. read as the corner `B`-module `B ⊗_{A_σ} N`), the tensor base

561	`pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChanged_pushforward_iso_tilde`.  Project-local; blueprint
562	`lem:coverinter_baseChanged_module_iso_tensor` (module core). -/
563	noncomputable def coverInter_baseChanged_sections_iso_tensor {R A R' B : CommRingCat.{u}}
564	    (φ : R ⟶ A) (ψ : R ⟶ R') (ρ : A ⟶ B) (σ' : R' ⟶ B)

1156	    (f : X ⟶ S) (g : S' ⟶ S) (f' : X' ⟶ S') (g' : X' ⟶ X)
1157	    (h : IsPullback g' f' f g) [QuasiCompact f] [IsSeparated f]
1158	    [IsAffine S] [IsAffine S']
1159	    (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] [∀ i, IsAffine (𝒰.X i)]
1160	    [Finite ((Scheme.Pullback.openCoverOfLeft 𝒰 f g).pushforwardIso
1161	      h.isoPullback.symm.hom).I₀]
1162	    [∀ i, IsAffine (((Scheme.Pullback.openCoverOfLeft 𝒰 f g).pushforwardIso
1163	      h.isoPullback.symm.hom).X i)]
1164	    (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent) :
1165	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj
1166	        (CechComplex f 𝒰 F)
1167	      ≅ CechComplex f'
1168	          ((Scheme.Pullback.openCoverOfLeft 𝒰 f g).pushforwardIso h.isoPullback.symm.hom)
1169	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj F) :=
1170	  -- Reduced (iter-304) to the factoring lemma: the homology/differential plumbing is
1171	  -- discharged, so the SOLE residual obligation is the cosimplicial Beck–Chevalley iso `e`
1172	  -- (`g^* ∘ f_* ∘ nerve ≅ f'_* ∘ g'^* ∘ nerve'`). Decomposed (iter-315): `e` is supplied by
1173	  -- `cechComplex_baseChange_cosimplicialIso`, the whiskered composite of the Beck–Chevalley
1174	  -- natural iso `cech_pushforward_baseChange_natIso` with the twisted-nerve identification

1176	  -- blueprinted residuals — the genuine open content of Stacks 02KG/02KH.
1177	  cechComplex_baseChange_iso_of_cosimplicialIso f g f' g' h 𝒰 F
1178	    (cechComplex_baseChange_cosimplicialIso f g f' g' h 𝒰 F hF)
1179	
1180	/-- **Flat base change for the Čech higher direct images** (Stacks 02KH,
1181	`lemma-flat-base-change-cohomology`).
1182	
1183	Given the cartesian square
1184	```
1185	  X' --g'--> X
1186	  |f'        |f
1187	  v          v
1188	  S' --g---> S
1189	```
1190	with `f` separated and quasi-compact, `F` quasi-coherent, `F' = (g')^* F`, and
1191	`g` flat, for every `i ≥ 0` the canonical base-change map between the
1192	unconditional Čech higher direct images is an isomorphism
1193	```
1194	  g^*(Rⁱ f_* F) ≅ Rⁱ f'_* ((g')^* F).
1195	```
1196	Equivalently, for `S = Spec A`, `S' = Spec B` with `A → B` flat, the comparison
1197	`Hⁱ(X, F) ⊗_A B → Hⁱ(X', F')` of `B`-modules is an isomorphism.
1198	
1199	We state the isomorphism as `Nonempty (… ≅ …)`; `𝒰` is a finite affine open cover of `X`,
1200	and the cover of `X' = X ×_S S'` used on the right is its canonical base change along `g'`
1201	(`Scheme.Pullback.openCoverOfLeft 𝒰 f g` transported to `X'` via `IsPullback.isoPullback`). -/
1202	theorem cech_flatBaseChange
1203	    (f : X ⟶ S) (g : S' ⟶ S) (f' : X' ⟶ S') (g' : X' ⟶ X)
1204	    (h : IsPullback g' f' f g) [Flat g] [QuasiCompact f] [IsSeparated f]
1205	    [IsAffine S] [IsAffine S']
1206	    (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] [∀ i, IsAffine (𝒰.X i)]
1207	    [Finite ((Scheme.Pullback.openCoverOfLeft 𝒰 f g).pushforwardIso
1208	      h.isoPullback.symm.hom).I₀]
1209	    [∀ i, IsAffine (((Scheme.Pullback.openCoverOfLeft 𝒰 f g).pushforwardIso
1210	      h.isoPullback.symm.hom).X i)]
1211	    (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent) (i : ℕ) :
1212	    Nonempty ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj (cechHigherDirectImage f 𝒰 F i) ≅
1213	      cechHigherDirectImage f'
1214	        ((Scheme.Pullback.openCoverOfLeft 𝒰 f g).pushforwardIso h.isoPullback.symm.hom)
1215	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj F) i) := by
1216	  -- Re-wired (iter-304): the assembly is now sorry-free *modulo* the single load-bearing
1217	  -- leaf-2 iso `cechComplex_baseChange_iso`. The two-step composite is:
1218	  --   (1) `pullback_mapHC_homologyIso` (flat exactness, complex level) commuting `g^*` with
1219	  --       Čech homology, and (2) `homologyMapIso` of the tensorial base-change iso
1220	  --       `cechComplex_baseChange_iso`. `cechHigherDirectImage = (CechComplex …).homology i`
1221	  --       definitionally, so the two endpoints match up to `rfl`.
1222	  exact ⟨(pullback_mapHC_homologyIso g (CechComplex f 𝒰 F) i).symm ≪≫
1223	    HomologicalComplex.homologyMapIso (cechComplex_baseChange_iso f g f' g' h 𝒰 F hF) i⟩
1224	
1225	end AlgebraicGeometry

700	noncomputable def pullback_spec_tilde_iso {R R' : CommRingCat.{u}}
701	    (φ : R ⟶ R') (M : ModuleCat.{u} R) :
702	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (Spec.map φ)).obj (tilde M) ≅
703	      tilde ((ModuleCat.extendScalars φ.hom).obj M) :=
704	  let adjL := (tilde.adjunction (R := R)).comp
705	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (Spec.map φ))
706	  let adjR := (ModuleCat.extendRestrictScalarsAdj φ.hom).comp (tilde.adjunction (R := R'))
707	  (((conjugateIsoEquiv adjL adjR).symm (gammaPushforwardNatIso φ)).symm).app M
708

710

712	isomorphism `e : g^* ∘ f_* ≅ f'_* ∘ (g')^*` by gluing affine-local isomorphisms, instead of
713	proving the canonical adjoint mate `pushforwardBaseChangeMap` is an isomorphism (which walls on the
714	mate ↔ `cancelBaseChange` coherence). The affine-local brick is the floor of that construction:
715	over the fully affine pushout square
716	```
717	  Spec (A ⊗_R R') --Spec ρ--> Spec A
718	  |Spec σ                      |Spec φ
719	  v                            v
720	  Spec R'      ----Spec ψ---->  Spec R
721	```
722	(`φ : R → A`, `ψ : R → R'`, `ρ = includeLeft`, `σ = includeRight` into `A ⊗_R R'`) it produces the
723	termwise isomorphism `g^*(f_* M̃) ≅ f'_*((g')^* M̃)`, assembled from the two sorry-free tilde
724	dictionaries `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` / `pullback_spec_tilde_iso` and the pure
725	commutative-algebra cancellation `Algebra.IsPushout.cancelBaseChange`, never forming the adjoint
726	mate.

729	`(extendScalars ψ) ∘ (restrictScalars φ) ≅ (restrictScalars σ) ∘ (extendScalars ρ)` realising
730	`R' ⊗_R M ≅ (A ⊗_R R') ⊗_A M` — was PROVEN to typecheck axiom-clean in the **CommRingCat framing**
731	(ambient algebras supplied as `φ.hom.toAlgebra` / `ψ.hom.toAlgebra`), by `@`-threading the
732	`RingHom.toAlgebra` instances through `Algebra.IsPushout.cancelBaseChange` (so its output is defeq to
733	the `compHom`-based `ModuleCat.extendScalars`/`restrictScalars` objects) with `IsPushout` transported
734	from `TensorProduct.isPushout'` via `Algebra.algebra_ext`. See `task_results` for the full working
735	construction. It is NOT yet landed as a top-level declaration: stating the tensor `A ⊗_R R'` in the
736	signature forces a Type-u framing whose *opaque* ambient `[Algebra R A]`/`[Algebra R R']` lets the
737	GLOBAL `Algebra.TensorProduct.leftAlgebra`/`rightAlgebra` instances win over the `toAlgebra` `letI`s,
738	breaking the `compHom`-defeq on the inner `Module A (A⊗_R R')` and outer `Module R' (·)`. The
739	recommended fix (iter-306) is the abstract-`B` framing: take `ρ : A ⟶ B`, `σ : R' ⟶ B` and a
740	`CategoryTheory.IsPushout φ ψ ρ σ` (or `IsPullback` of the Spec square) hypothesis, derive
741	`Algebra.IsPushout` in the proof with all algebras `toAlgebra` (no global tensor instances to shadow),
742	and assemble the 5-step chain. See blueprint `lem:affine_pushforward_pullback_baseChange`. -/
743	
744	/-- **Module-level base-change cancellation iso (abstract-`B` framing).** Given a pushout square of
745	commutative rings
746	```
747	  R --φ--> A
748	  |ψ       |ρ
749	  v        v
750	  R' --σ-> B
751	```
752	(so `B ≅ A ⊗_R R'`) and an `A`-module `M`, the `R'`-linear isomorphism
753	`extendScalars_ψ (restrictScalars_φ M) ≅ restrictScalars_σ (extendScalars_ρ M)`, i.e.
754	`R' ⊗_R M ≅ restrictScalars_σ (B ⊗_A M)`. It realises the inverse of Mathlib's
755	`Algebra.IsPushout.cancelBaseChange` between the extension-of-scalars objects attached to the two
756	legs of the square. The abstract `B` (rather than the concrete `A ⊗_R R'`) keeps all ambient
757	algebra instances `toAlgebra`, so no global tensor-product instance shadows them and
758	`cancelBaseChange`'s output module structures land defeq on the `compHom`-based
759	`extendScalars`/`restrictScalars` objects. Project-local: the module core of the affine termwise
760	base-change brick. See blueprint `lem:baseChangeCancelModuleIso`. -/
761	noncomputable def baseChangeCancelModuleIso {R A R' B : CommRingCat.{u}}
762	    (φ : R ⟶ A) (ψ : R ⟶ R') (ρ : A ⟶ B) (σ : R' ⟶ B)
763	    (h : CategoryTheory.IsPushout φ ψ ρ σ) (M : ModuleCat.{u} A) :
764	    (ModuleCat.extendScalars ψ.hom).obj ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom).obj M) ≅
765	      (ModuleCat.restrictScalars σ.hom).obj ((ModuleCat.extendScalars ρ.hom).obj M) := by
766	  letI iRA : Algebra (R : Type u) (A : Type u) := φ.hom.toAlgebra
767	  letI iRR' : Algebra (R : Type u) (R' : Type u) := ψ.hom.toAlgebra
768	  letI iAB : Algebra (A : Type u) (B : Type u) := ρ.hom.toAlgebra
769	  letI iR'B : Algebra (R' : Type u) (B : Type u) := σ.hom.toAlgebra
770	  letI iRB : Algebra (R : Type u) (B : Type u) := (ρ.hom.comp φ.hom).toAlgebra
771	  letI iMR : Module (R : Type u) M := ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom).obj M).isModule
772	  haveI tRAM : IsScalarTower (R : Type u) A M :=
773	    ⟨fun r a m => by rw [Algebra.smul_def, mul_smul]; rfl⟩
774	  haveI tRAB : @IsScalarTower (R : Type u) A B _ iAB.toSMul iRB.toSMul :=
775	    @IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq (R : Type u) A B _ _ _ iRA iAB iRB (fun _ => rfl)
776	  haveI tRR'B : @IsScalarTower (R : Type u) R' B _ iR'B.toSMul iRB.toSMul :=
777	    @IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq (R : Type u) R' B _ _ _ iRR' iR'B iRB (fun r => by
778	      change (ρ.hom.comp φ.hom) r = σ.hom (ψ.hom r)
779	      have := congrArg (fun m => (CommRingCat.Hom.hom m) r) h.w
780	      simpa using this)
781	  haveI hpush : @Algebra.IsPushout (R : Type u) R' _ _ iRR' A B _ _ iRA iR'B iAB iRB tRAB tRR'B :=
782	    (CommRingCat.isPushout_iff_isPushout).mp h.flip
783	  exact LinearEquiv.toModuleIso
784	    (@Algebra.IsPushout.cancelBaseChange (R : Type u) R' _ _ iRR' A B _ _ iRA iRB iAB iR'B
785	      tRAB tRR'B hpush M _ iMR _ tRAM).symm
786	
787	/-- **Affine termwise base change (abstract-`B` framing).** For a pushout square of commutative
788	rings `(φ : R ⟶ A, ψ : R ⟶ R', ρ : A ⟶ B, σ : R' ⟶ B)` — equivalently the cartesian square of
789	affine schemes obtained by `Spec` — and an `A`-module `M`, the base-change comparison
790	`g^*(f_* M̃) ≅ f'_*((g')^* M̃)` for `f = Spec φ`, `g = Spec ψ`, `f' = Spec σ`, `g' = Spec ρ`. It is
791	assembled as a 5-step chain from the two affine tilde dictionaries
792	(`pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` / `pullback_spec_tilde_iso`) and the module-level cancellation core
793	`baseChangeCancelModuleIso`, and it **never** forms the adjoint mate
794	`pushforwardBaseChangeMap`, so it sidesteps the unresolved mate ↔ `cancelBaseChange` coherence
795	obligation of the canonical route. This is the affine-local brick (the floor) of the active
796	concrete-tilde route to flat base change (Stacks 02KG/02KH). Project-local. See blueprint
797	`lem:affine_pushforward_pullback_baseChange`. -/
798	noncomputable def affinePushforwardPullbackBaseChange {R A R' B : CommRingCat.{u}}
799	    (φ : R ⟶ A) (ψ : R ⟶ R') (ρ : A ⟶ B) (σ : R' ⟶ B)
800	    (h : CategoryTheory.IsPushout φ ψ ρ σ) (M : ModuleCat.{u} A) :
801	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (Spec.map ψ)).obj
802	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ)).obj (tilde M)) ≅
803	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map σ)).obj
804	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Spec.map ρ)).obj (tilde M)) :=
805	  (Scheme.Modules.pullback (Spec.map ψ)).mapIso (pushforward_spec_tilde_iso φ M)
806	    ≪≫ pullback_spec_tilde_iso ψ ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom).obj M)
807	    ≪≫ (tilde.functor R').mapIso (baseChangeCancelModuleIso φ ψ ρ σ h M)
808	    ≪≫ (pushforward_spec_tilde_iso σ ((ModuleCat.extendScalars ρ.hom).obj M)).symm
809	    ≪≫ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map σ)).mapIso (pullback_spec_tilde_iso ρ M).symm
810	
811	/-- **Affine base change.** If `f` is an affine morphism and the square is
812	cartesian, then the base-change map for the pushforward is an isomorphism. In the
813	affine-local picture this is the associativity isomorphism
814	`(R' ⊗_R A) ⊗_A M ≅ R' ⊗_R M`, which needs no flatness.
815	
816	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma "Affine base change". -/
817	/- USER: UPSTREAM gap for Stacks 02KG/02KH `cechComplex_baseChange_iso`
818	   (`Cohomology/CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean`) and a PREREQUISITE of Kleiman 4.8
819	   Step-1 strata-openness. Close via the CONCRETE-TILDE dictionary, NOT the adjoint-mate
820	   route (walled ~30 iters in FBC-B): the sorry-free `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` /
821	   `pullback_spec_tilde_iso` + `regroupEquiv` + `TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange`
822	   give the affine-local iso. Two obligations remain: (1) the affine reduction (locality on
823	   S and S'), (2) the mate ↔ `cancelBaseChange` identification. Reference: Stacks Project,
824	   "Affine base change" + Tag 02KH. -/
825	theorem affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso (h : IsPullback g' f' f g) [IsAffineHom f]
826	    (F : X.Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent] :
827	    IsIso (pushforwardBaseChangeMap f g f' g' h.w F) := by
828	  -- A morphism of sheaves of modules is an isomorphism iff it is an isomorphism

830	  -- `Modules.isIso_iff_isIso_app_affineOpens`; the affine opens form a basis).

832	  -- tilde dictionary computes the section map.
833	  rw [Modules.isIso_iff_isIso_app_affineOpens]
834	  intro U
835	  -- Remaining goal: `IsIso (Hom.app (pushforwardBaseChangeMap …) U)` for `U` affine.

838	  --   • pushforward — `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso φ M : (Spec φ)_* (M̃) ≅ (restrictScalars φ M)~`
839	  --     (closed iter-241);
840	  --   • pullback — `pullback_spec_tilde_iso φ M : (Spec φ)^* (M̃) ≅ (extendScalars φ M)~`
841	  --     (closed this iter, via the uniqueness-of-left-adjoints route
842	  --     `conjugateIsoEquiv` over the right-adjoint natural iso `gammaPushforwardNatIso`).
843	  --   • the pure ring-theoretic target `TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange`
844	  --     `(R' ⊗_R A) ⊗_A M ≃ R' ⊗_R M` (no flatness) is in Mathlib.
845	  --
846	  -- TWO obligations remain for the unconditional general theorem, both Mathlib-absent and each a
847	  -- multi-hundred-LOC build (no quick axiom-clean sub-step — even an affine-affine version of the
848	  -- statement still needs obligation (2)):
849	  --
850	  -- (1) THE AFFINE REDUCTION. The schemes `S, S', X, X'` here are ARBITRARY (only `f` is affine,
851	  --     `F` quasi-coherent). The Stacks proof "the statement is local on `S` and `S'`" reduces to
852	  --     `S = Spec R`, `S' = Spec R'`, `X = Spec A`, `F = M̃`. Carrying that reduction out in Lean
853	  --     requires the naturality / base-change compatibility of `pushforwardBaseChangeMap` with
854	  --     restriction to affine opens of `S'` (and a choice of affine `V = Spec R ⊆ S` containing the
855	  --     image of `U = Spec R'` under `g`, over which `f`-affineness gives `X_V = Spec A`). This
856	  --     base-change-of-the-base-change-map compatibility is itself not packaged in Mathlib.
857	  -- (2) THE ADJOINT-MATE ↔ `cancelBaseChange` IDENTIFICATION. Once everything is affine, transporting
858	  --     the abstract adjoint-mate `pushforwardBaseChangeMap` through the four dictionary isos must be
859	  --     shown to equal `tilde (cancelBaseChange)`. This is a coherence computation unwinding the
860	  --     mate of the `((g')^*, (g')_*)`-unit; it is the genuine crux named in the blueprint and has no
861	  --     ready-made Mathlib counterpart.
862	  --
863	  -- Both are deferred (documented partial, per the iter-242 objective). The `#37189` bump
864	  -- (`isIso_fromTildeΓ_pushforward`) is the recorded fallback if the in-tree affine close walls.
865	  -- See `informal/affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso.md`.
866	  sorry
867	
868	/-- **Flat base change, `i = 0` case.** If `g` is flat and `f` is quasi-compact
869	and quasi-separated, then the base-change map for the pushforward is an
870	isomorphism. Equivalently, in the affine situation `S = Spec A`, `S' = Spec B`
871	with `A → B` flat, the comparison map `H⁰(X, F) ⊗_A B → H⁰(X_B, F_B)` is an
872	isomorphism.

875	theorem flatBaseChange_pushforward_isIso (h : IsPullback g' f' f g) [Flat g]
876	    [QuasiCompact f] [QuasiSeparated f] (F : X.Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent] :
877	    IsIso (pushforwardBaseChangeMap f g f' g' h.w F) := by
878	  -- Proof strategy (Stacks 02KH, `i = 0`), deferred to a later iteration:
879	  -- the statement is local on `S'`, so reduce to `S = Spec A`, `S' = Spec B`
880	  -- with `A → B` flat.  Choose a finite affine open cover `𝒰` of `X`.  Since `f`
881	  -- is quasi-compact and quasi-separated the Čech complex of `𝒰` computes
882	  -- `H⁰(X, F)`, and base change identifies `Čech(𝒰_B, F_B) ≅ Čech(𝒰, F) ⊗_A B`
883	  -- term by term via `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso`.  Flatness of `A → B`
884	  -- makes `- ⊗_A B` exact, so it commutes with `H⁰`, giving the isomorphism
885	  -- `H⁰(X, F) ⊗_A B ≅ H⁰(X_B, F_B)`.  Needs the (missing) Čech-cohomology /
886	  -- affine-cover infrastructure for `SheafOfModules`; see
887	  -- `informal/affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso.md`.
888	  sorry
889	
890	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — cancellation/localization compatibility
891	
892	For a tower of commutative rings `R → S → S'` and a further homomorphism `R → A`, the
893	cancellation isomorphism `Algebra.IsPushout.cancelBaseChange` for the `S'`-tower is the
894	base change along `S → S'` of the cancellation isomorphism for the `S`-tower. This is the
895	purely commutative-algebraic "cancellation move" of the affine-restriction naturality square
896	(`pushforwardPullbackBaseChange_restrict_naturality`); see blueprint
897	`lem:cancelBaseChange_localization_compat`. It carries no scheme scaffolding.
